raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the proposed framework is conceptually coherent.","R1.2":"Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes checking whether the argument from method design to claimed advantages is logically consistent.","R1.3":"The artifact makes performance and transfer claims that should be judged against the reported datasets, comparisons, and metrics.","R1.4":"Because the text presents CycleSenseNet as distinct from conventional and named prior approaches, assessing novelty is central to the implied evaluation.","R1.6":"An open-ended reaction to a paper-style passage naturally includes sentence-level clarity, register, concision, and readability.","R1.7":"The artifact names prior methods and datasets, so attribution, coverage, and reference adequacy are relevant to the requested judgment.","RX.1":"Any projection of broad feedback must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The responsibility includes guarding against endorsing broad deployment, generality, or superiority claims beyond the provided evidence.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually critiquing or revising the artifact.","RX.4":"Assessment responsibilities should account for downstream risks from overstated battery-health claims in deployment contexts.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the proposed framework is conceptually coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the artifact centrally includes checking whether the argument from method design to claimed advantages is logically consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes performance and transfer claims that should be judged against the reported datasets, comparisons, and metrics.
rationale.R1.4: Because the text presents CycleSenseNet as distinct from conventional and named prior approaches, assessing novelty is central to the implied evaluation.
rationale.R1.6: An open-ended reaction to a paper-style passage naturally includes sentence-level clarity, register, concision, and readability.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact names prior methods and datasets, so attribution, coverage, and reference adequacy are relevant to the requested judgment.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of broad feedback must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The responsibility includes guarding against endorsing broad deployment, generality, or superiority claims beyond the provided evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually critiquing or revising the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Assessment responsibilities should account for downstream risks from overstated battery-health claims in deployment contexts.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
